parameters.query: SELECT key, content FROM soul ORDER BY key
SELECT key, content FROM agents ORDER BY key
SELECT name, display_name, timezone, preferences, context, setup_done FROM user_profiles WHERE user_id = 'telegram:{{ $('Merge Input').first().json.userId }}' LIMIT 1
SELECT role, content FROM conversations WHERE session_id = 'telegram:{{ $('Merge Input').first().json.chatId }}' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 20
SELECT server_name, path, mcp_url, description, tools FROM mcp_registry WHERE active = true ORDER BY server_name

INSERT INTO conversations (session_id, user_id, role, content) VALUES ('{{ $json.sessionId }}', '{{ $json.userId }}', 'user', '{{ $json.userMessage.replace(/'/g, "''").replace(/\$/g, '$$') }}'); INSERT INTO conversations (session_id, user_id, role, content) VALUES ('{{ $json.sessionId }}', '{{ $json.userId }}', 'assistant', '{{ $json.assistantMessage.replace(/'/g, "''").replace(/\$/g, '$$') }}'); {{ $json.markSetupDone ? "UPDATE user_profiles SET setup_done = true WHERE user_id = 'telegram:" + $json.userId + "';" : '' }}

INSERT INTO memory_daily (date, content, role, user_id) VALUES (CURRENT_DATE, '{{ ($json.userMessage + ' → ' + $json.assistantMessage).replace(/'/g, "''").replace(/\$/g, '$$') }}', 'interaction', '{{ $json.userId }}')

parameters.description: Search long-term memory. Input: a search query string.
Save important info to long-term memory. Input: JSON with content (string), category (general/decision/preference/contact/project), importance (1-10).
Make HTTP requests. Input: JSON with url (string) and optional method (GET/POST).
Search the web. Input: search query as string or JSON with q field. Returns top 5 results with title, URL, and snippet.

Read a webpage and return its content as clean markdown. Input: URL string or JSON with url field. Use this instead of HTTP Tool when you need to read webpage content (articles, docs, blog posts). Returns clean text without HTML boilerplate.
List or manage n8n workflows. Input: JSON with action (list/get/create) and optional workflowId.

Set a timed reminder or scheduled task. Two modes:
- Reminder (type='reminder'): sends a Telegram message at the given time. Use for 'remind me at/in...'.
- Task (type='task'): the agent executes the instructions at the given time and sends the result. Use for 'do X at Y o'clock', 'fetch/search/summarize X at Y'.
Input: time (ISO 8601), message (text describing reminder or task instructions), type ('reminder' or 'task', default 'reminder').

Builds new n8n workflows for general automations and integrations. Use when the user explicitly wants to automate something or build an integration workflow. NOT for MCP servers — use mcp_builder for that. NOT as a fallback when other tools fail. Input: JSON with 'task' (description of the automation to build).

parameters.name: mcp_builder
parameters.description: Builds a new MCP Server workflow in n8n. ALWAYS use this when the user wants to build an MCP server, MCP tool, or API integration as MCP. NEVER use WorkflowBuilder for MCP. On error: report the error, do NOT fall back to WorkflowBuilder. Input: JSON with 'task' (description of what the server should do). After build: user must deactivate + reactivate the workflow in n8n UI (webhook registration bug).

Calls a tool on an MCP server. Use when the user asks something that an available MCP server can answer (weather, book lookup, etc.). Required parameters: mcp_url (server URL), tool_name (tool to call), arguments (JSON object with parameters). Check mcp_registry table for available servers and their tools.

parameters.name: library_manager

parameters.query: SELECT name, status, updated_at FROM projects WHERE status = 'active' ORDER BY updated_at DESC LIMIT 20
